stem: According to the 2026 RISC guidance, which statement about confirming dosage is correct?

options.text: Short-half-life radioisotopes are preferred to reduce patient dose and prolonged exposure.
Short-half-life radioisotopes are not preferred to reduce patient dose and prolonged exposure.
The guidance treats this as optional and leaves the decision entirely to individual preference.
The rule applies only to nonmedical industrial facilities, not to clinical nuclear radiology.

explanation: Correct answer: A. Short-half-life radioisotopes are preferred to reduce patient dose and prolonged exposure. This point is drawn from Chapter 5, section '5.1 Confirming dosage', and is tested because it is a practical safety rule, regulatory requirement, high-yield distinction, or commonly confused threshold.
